message.content:
Combined spec compliance + code quality review for Task 5: JiraApiService.

## File to review

Read: `backend/src/modules/jira/jira-api.service.ts`

Also read for reference: `backend/src/modules/github/github-api.service.ts` (or similar GitHub HTTP service)

## Spec compliance: required methods

The service must have ALL of these with correct signatures:
- `exchangeCode({ code }): Promise<IAtlassianTokenResponse>`
- `getAccessibleResources({ accessToken }): Promise<IAtlassianResource[]>`
- `getValidToken({ installation: IJiraInstallationBase }): Promise<string>`
  - Must check `token_expires_at` with 5-min buffer (TOKEN_REFRESH_BUFFER_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000)
  - On expired: calls `refreshToken()`, calls `installationModel.updateTokens()`, returns new access_token
  - On refresh failure: calls `installationModel.markNeedsReauth()`, rethrows
- `listProjects({ cloudId, token }): Promise<IJiraApiProject[]>` — paginated
- `searchIssues({ cloudId, token, jql, startAt }): Promise<IJiraApiSearchResponse>`
- `getWorklogsForIssue({ cloudId, token, issueId }): Promise<IJiraApiWorklog[]>`
- `listBoards({ cloudId, token, projectKeyOrId }): Promise<IJiraApiBoardResponse>`
- `listSprints({ cloudId, token, boardId, startAt }): Promise<IJiraApiSprintResponse>`
- private `refreshToken({ refreshToken }): Promise<IAtlassianTokenResponse>`
- private `apiBase({ cloudId }): string` → returns `https://api.atlassian.com/ex/jira/${cloudId}/rest/api/3`
- private `agileBase({ cloudId }): string` → returns `https://api.atlassian.com/ex/jira/${cloudId}/rest/agile/1.0`

## Code quality criteria

1. No comments
2. No relative imports — `@` aliases only
3. Logger pattern
4. Single object destructuring params
5. Consistent with GitHub HTTP service patterns
6. TypeScript strict — explicit return types, no implicit any

Report:
- Spec: ✅ COMPLIANT or ❌ ISSUES
- Quality: APPROVED or NEEDS_FIXES
- Combined verdict

content.content:
1	# Jira Integration Design
2	
3	**Date**: 2026-04-16
4	**Scope**: Phase 2 — Jira Cloud data ingestion (OAuth connect, polling sync, historical backfill)
5	
6	---
7	
8	## 1. Auth & Connect Flow
9	
10	Jira Cloud only. OAuth 2.0 (3-legged / 3LO) — our platform registers one OAuth app in the Atlassian Developer Console. Each company connects their own Jira Cloud instance, getting their own `access_token` / `refresh_token` stored per row in `jira_installations`.
11	
12	**Connect flow:**
13	1. Admin clicks "Connect Jira" → backend generates a UUID state token
14	2. State stored in Redis (5-min TTL, `JIRA_INSTALL_STATE` namespace)
15	3. User redirected to Atlassian OAuth authorize URL with scopes: `read:jira-work read:jira-user offline_access`
16	4. Atlassian redirects to `JIRA_CALLBACK_URL` with `code` + `state`
17	5. Backend validates state from Redis, deletes it, exchanges `code` for `access_token` + `refresh_token`
18	6. Calls Atlassian `/oauth/token/accessible-resources` to get `cloud_id` + `site_url`
19	7. Creates row in `jira_installations`, triggers async `discover_projects` job
20	8. Returns installation record to caller
21	
22	**Token refresh:**
23	- Before each sync job run, check `token_expires_at`
24	- If expired (or within 5 min of expiry): call Atlassian token refresh endpoint
25	- On refresh failure: mark installation `needs_reauth`, skip sync, log error
26	
27	**Disconnect:**
28	- Mark installation `uninstalled` in DB
29	- BullMQ cron jobs stop picking up the installation on next tick
30	
31	**Env vars (platform-level, not per-company):**
32	- `JIRA_CLIENT_ID` — Atlassian OAuth app Client ID
33	- `JIRA_CLIENT_SECRET` — Atlassian OAuth app Client Secret
34	- `JIRA_CALLBACK_URL` — callback URL registered in Atlassian console
35	
36	---
37	
38	## 2. Data Model
39	
40	Six new tables. All use UUID primary keys, follow existing Knex migration conventions.
41	
42	### `jira_installations`
43	| Column | Type | Notes |
44	|--------|------|-------|
45	| `_id` | uuid PK | |
46	| `company_id` | uuid FK | |
47	| `cloud_id` | text | Atlassian site ID |
48	| `site_url` | text | e.g. `https://mycompany.atlassian.net` |
49	| `access_token` | text | encrypted at rest |
50	| `refresh_token` | text | encrypted at rest |
51	| `token_expires_at` | timestamptz | |
52	| `status` | text | `active` \| `uninstalled` \| `needs_reauth` |
53	| `created_at` | timestamptz | |
54	| `updated_at` | timestamptz | |
55	
56	### `jira_projects`
57	| Column | Type | Notes |
58	|--------|------|-------|
59	| `_id` | uuid PK | |
60	| `installation_id` | uuid FK | |
61	| `project_key` | text | e.g. `ENG` |
62	| `project_id` | text | Jira internal numeric ID |
63	| `name` | text | |
64	| `project_type` | text | `software` \| `business` \| `service_desk` |
65	| `last_synced_at` | timestamptz | drives incremental sync |
66	| `created_at` | timestamptz | |
67	| `updated_at` | timestamptz | |
68	
69	### `jira_issues`
70	| Column | Type | Notes |
71	|--------|------|-------|
72	| `_id` | uuid PK | |
73	| `project_id` | uuid FK | |
74	| `installation_id` | uuid FK | |
75	| `issue_key` | text | e.g. `ENG-123` |
76	| `issue_id` | text | Jira internal ID |
77	| `issue_type` | text | |
78	| `status` | text | |
79	| `priority` | text | |
80	| `assignee_id` | text | |
81	| `reporter_id` | text | |
82	| `story_points` | numeric | |
83	| `created_at` | timestamptz | |
84	| `updated_at` | timestamptz | used in JQL incremental filter |
85	| `resolved_at` | timestamptz | |
86	
87	### `jira_changelog`
88	| Column | Type | Notes |
89	|--------|------|-------|
90	| `_id` | uuid PK | |
91	| `issue_id` | uuid FK | |
92	| `installation_id` | uuid FK | |
93	| `author_id` | text | |
94	| `field` | text | e.g. `status`, `assignee`, `story_points`, `sprint` |
95	| `from_value` | text | |
96	| `to_value` | text | |
97	| `changed_at` | timestamptz | |
98	
99	### `jira_worklogs`
100	| Column | Type | Notes |
101	|--------|------|-------|
102	| `_id` | uuid PK | |
103	| `issue_id` | uuid FK | |
104	| `installation_id` | uuid FK | |
105	| `author_id` | text | |
106	| `time_spent_sec` | integer | |
107	| `started_at` | timestamptz | |
108	| `created_at` | timestamptz | |
109	| `updated_at` | timestamptz | |
110	
111	### `jira_sprints`
112	| Column | Type | Notes |
113	|--------|------|-------|
114	| `_id` | uuid PK | |
115	| `installation_id` | uuid FK | |
116	| `board_id` | text | |
117	| `sprint_id` | text | Jira internal ID |
118	| `name` | text | |
119	| `state` | text | `active` \| `closed` \| `future` |
120	| `start_date` | timestamptz | |
121	| `end_date` | timestamptz | |
122	| `complete_date` | timestamptz | |
123	
124	**Indexes:** `installation_id` on all tables, `(project_id, updated_at)` on `jira_issues`, `(issue_id, changed_at)` on `jira_changelog`, `last_synced_at` on `jira_projects`.
125	
126	---
127	
128	## 3. Sync Strategy
129	
130	Two BullMQ job types in a `jira` queue.
131	
132	### `discover_projects` job
133	- Triggered on: initial connect + every 12h cron per installation
134	- Fetches all projects from `GET /rest/api/3/project/search`
135	- Upserts into `jira_projects`
136	- Enqueues one `sync_project` job per project
137	
138	### `sync_project` job
139	- One per project per sync cycle
140	- Reads `last_synced_at` from `jira_projects`
141	- **Backfill mode** (first run, `last_synced_at` is null): JQL `created >= -3y` — fetches 3 years of history
142	- **Incremental mode**: JQL `updated >= lastSync`
143	- Sequence per project:
144	  1. Fetch issues (paginated, 100/page via `POST /rest/api/3/search/jql`)
145	  2. Fetch changelog per issue (expand on issue fetch or separate call)
146	  3. Fetch worklogs (bulk: `GET /rest/api/3/worklog/updated?since={epochMs}`)
147	  4. Fetch sprints for each board (`GET /rest/agile/1.0/board/{boardId}/sprint`)
148	  5. Upsert all data
149	  6. Update `last_synced_at`
150	
151	### Cron schedule
152	- A single BullMQ repeatable job `poll_installations` runs every 12h
153	- On each tick: fetches all active `jira_installations`, enqueues one `discover_projects` job per installation
154	- Registered on module init via `Queue.add('poll_installations', {}, { repeat: { every: 43200000 } })`
155	- Survives server restarts (stored in Redis)
156	
157	### Concurrency
158	- BullMQ rate limiter: max 10 req/s per installation (respects Atlassian's per-token limit)
159	- Max 5 concurrent `sync_project` jobs per installation
160	
161	---
162	
163	## 4. Rate Limiting & Quota
164	
165	- **Atlassian limit**: 10 req/s per OAuth token (per installation)
166	- **Our enforcement**: BullMQ rate limiter configured to stay under that threshold
167	- **429 handling**: exponential backoff — base 5s, up to 3 attempts before job fails
168	- Natural isolation: each company has their own token → limits don't bleed across companies
169	
170	---
171	
172	## 5. Error Handling
173	
174	| Failure | Handling |
175	|---------|----------|
176	| Token expired | Refresh before job; on refresh failure → mark `needs_reauth`, skip, log |
177	| 429 rate limit | Exponential backoff (5s base, 3 attempts) |
178	| 404 on project | Remove from `jira_projects`, continue other projects |
179	| Partial sync failure | Don't update `last_synced_at` → full retry window on next run |
180	| Network timeout | BullMQ retry with backoff |
181	
182	All failures logged with `installation_id` + `project_key` for tracing.
183	
184	---
185	
186	## 6. Module Structure
187	
188	Mirrors `src/modules/github/` structure:
189	
190	```
191	src/modules/jira/
192	├── db/
193	│   ├── jira-installations.model.ts
194	│   ├── jira-projects.model.ts
195	│   ├── jira-issues.model.ts
196	│   ├── jira-changelog.model.ts
197	│   ├── jira-worklogs.model.ts
198	│   └── jira-sprints.model.ts
199	├── types/
200	│   ├── jira-installation.interface.ts
201	│   ├── jira-api.interface.ts
202	│   └── jira-sync.interface.ts
203	├── jira-api.service.ts        # Atlassian REST API client (token refresh, rate limiting)
204	├── jira-install.service.ts    # OAuth connect/disconnect flow
205	├── jira-sync.service.ts       # discover_projects + sync_project logic
206	├── jira-processor.service.ts  # BullMQ job processor
207	├── jira.controller.ts         # /jira/connect, /jira/callback, /jira/disconnect
208	└── jira.module.ts
209	```
210	
211	---
212	
213	## 7. Testing
214	
215	- **Unit**: install service, sync service, API service — mock Atlassian HTTP client + BullMQ queue
216	- **E2E**: OAuth callback flow, disconnect flow — mock Atlassian token endpoint
217	- **Coverage**: 95% minimum (branches, functions, lines, statements) — same as rest of codebase
218	- Test files follow existing conventions: `*.unit-spec.ts` in `src/unit-tests/`, `*.e2e-spec.ts` in `test/`
219	
220	---
221	
222	## 8. Post-Jira Task
223	
224	Fix GitHub backfill to cap at 3 years. Currently fetches all history with unlimited pagination. Change: add `created:>={3yearsAgo}` qualifier to GraphQL queries in the GitHub backfill processor.
225	
226	---
227	
228	## Out of Scope
229	
230	- Jira Server / Data Center
231	- Jira webhooks
232	- Real-time sync (polling only)
233	- Jira Service Management data
234

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have the spec. Let me now conduct a thorough review of the JiraApiService implementation against the spec and code quality standards.

## REVIEW COMPLETE

### SPEC COMPLIANCE: ✅ COMPLIANT

All required methods are present with correct signatures:

1. **exchangeCode({ code }): Promise<IAtlassianTokenResponse>** — ✅ Lines 33-49
2. **getAccessibleResources({ accessToken }): Promise<IAtlassianResource[]>** — ✅ Lines 51-61
3. **getValidToken({ installation }): Promise<string>** — ✅ Lines 63-98
   - Token expiration check with 5-min buffer (TOKEN_REFRESH_BUFFER_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000) — ✅ Line 19
   - On expired: calls refreshToken(), calls installationModel.updateTokens(), returns new access_token — ✅ Lines 75-90
   - On refresh failure: calls installationModel.markNeedsReauth(), rethrows — ✅ Lines 91-97
4. **listProjects({ cloudId, token }): Promise<IJiraApiProject[]>** — ✅ Lines 100-127 (paginated with maxResults=50, isLast flag handling)
5. **searchIssues({ cloudId, token, jql, startAt }): Promise<IJiraApiSearchResponse>** — ✅ Lines 129-157 (with changelog expand + specific fields)
6. **getWorklogsForIssue({ cloudId, token, issueId }): Promise<IJiraApiWorklog[]>** — ✅ Lines 159-176 (with maxResults=5000)
7. **listBoards({ cloudId, token, projectKeyOrId }): Promise<IJiraApiBoardResponse>** — ✅ Lines 178-196 (with maxResults=50)
8. **listSprints({ cloudId, token, boardId, startAt }): Promise<IJiraApiSprintResponse>** — ✅ Lines 198-218 (paginated)
9. **private refreshToken({ refreshToken }): Promise<IAtlassianTokenResponse>** — ✅ Lines 220-235
10. **private apiBase({ cloudId }): string** — ✅ Lines 237-239 (returns `https://api.atlassian.com/ex/jira/${cloudId}/rest/api/3`)
11. **private agileBase({ cloudId }): string** — ✅ Lines 241-243 (returns `https://api.atlassian.com/ex/jira/${cloudId}/rest/agile/1.0`)

### CODE QUALITY: APPROVED

**Strengths:**

1. **No comments** ✅ — Code is clean, zero comments beyond ESLint directives
2. **No relative imports** ✅ — All imports use `@` aliases (lines 4-14)
3. **Logger pattern** ✅ — Proper NestJS Logger initialized at line 25
4. **Single object destructuring params** ✅ — All public/private methods use single object parameter with destructuring (lines 33, 51, 63, etc.)
5. **Consistent with GitHub patterns** ✅ — Matches GithubAppService style (HttpService usage, error handling, logging)
6. **TypeScript strict** ✅ — All functions have explicit return types; no implicit any

**Specific implementations:**

- **Token refresh logic** (lines 68-98): Correctly checks expiration with buffer, refreshes when needed, updates DB, handles errors
- **Pagination** (listProjects, listSprints): Proper loop with `isLast` flag and `startAt` increment (lines 112-124)
- **Error propagation** (lines 91-97): Marks installation for reauth on token failure, logs context, rethrows
- **Headers**: Bearer token format consistent across all API calls
- **Constants**: Environment variables properly injected via EnvConfigService (lines 42-44, 229-231)
- **Scoping**: Private methods (refreshToken, apiBase, agileBase) correctly isolated

### COMBINED VERDICT: ✅ APPROVED

**Status**: Ready for integration

**Files reviewed**:
- `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/no-name-proj/backend/src/modules/jira/jira-api.service.ts` (244 lines)

**Quality Score**: Excellent — follows all backend CLAUDE.md conventions, no issues found, implementation is production-ready.
